summary: US-844 visual regression verification found a laptop-width settlement blocker. Spexus current_context was read before task access, all required dependency reports were present and reviewed, npm run verify passed, and fresh Playwright/browser captures were created for active score, deal settlement, and final result states at 1280x800 desktop and 1024x768 laptop. Desktop active/settlement/final screenshots are framed and readable, active laptop and final laptop are readable, and the final state correctly exposes no continuation controls. However, output/playwright/mz-406-settlement-laptop.png clips the right side of the deal score-change table/result content at 1024px, including the final 'Стало' column area, so the no-clipped-score-results visual gate is not satisfied.

tests_run.evidence: Vitest: 13 files and 125 tests passed. Build: tsc and vite build completed successfully.
Vite served the app at http://127.0.0.1:5331/ after sandbox escalation. The server was stopped after browser capture.

Captured active, settlement, and final screenshots at 1280x800 and 1024x768. Script metrics reported no document-level horizontal overflow and showed final-state button arrays were empty; manual screenshot inspection still found settlement table clipping inside the 1024px result panel.

risks.risk: Laptop settlement result table is clipped at 1024x768.
risks.evidence: output/playwright/mz-406-settlement-laptop.png shows the right side of the score-change table cut off, including the 'Стало' column values. This violates the task expectation to confirm no clipped whist columns/numbers after the MZ-403 visual fix.

verification_environment.verification_scope: US-844 desktop/laptop visual regression for active score display, deal settlement, and final result states using existing npm verify/dev entrypoints plus bundled Playwright browser capture

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

verification_environment.process_cleanup: Local Vite dev server was stopped after screenshot capture.
verification_environment.notes: No production source code was changed. The only added non-report artifact is a Playwright capture helper under output/playwright/ plus generated PNG evidence.

visual_artifacts.notes: Existing artifact reviewed; despite filename, it shows deal-settlement state rather than an active game state.
Existing desktop settlement screenshot shows readable side score cards and result table at 1280 width.
Existing desktop final screenshot shows readable score cards, final ranking/settlement, and no action buttons.
Deterministic settlement fixture used as basis for fresh MZ-406 capture.
Deterministic final-result fixture used as basis for fresh MZ-406 capture.
Score cards and whist numbers are readable; action controls are visible and aligned.
Score cards, settlement summary, score-change table, whist line, and 'Подсчитать' control are readable.
Final result/ranking/settlement text is readable and no continuation controls are rendered.
Score cards and whist numbers remain readable at laptop width.
The result panel clips the right side of the score-change table, including the final column values.
Final result/ranking/settlement text is readable and no continuation controls are rendered.
Browser capture helper used to create the six fresh MZ-406 screenshots and layout metrics.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Active/game score display exposes bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist values for all three players across desktop and laptop widths.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Active state uses deterministic synthetic score fixture rendered through the live app.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Deal settlement and final result surfaces expose result summaries, score changes/ranking, and readable settlement text.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Final result surfaces pass, and desktop settlement passes, but laptop settlement clips the score-change table.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Given a deal completes, the score table/result display shows updated bullet, mountain, and mutual whist values with readable before/delta/after detail.
evidence_matrix.limitations: The laptop viewport clips the right side of the before/delta/after table, so the visual acceptance check is not satisfied.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Given the bullet target is reached, final ranking/result summary is shown and accidental continuation is prevented.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Verified by screenshot inspection plus capture metrics showing final-state button arrays were empty.

evidence_matrix.req_or_ac_or_contract: MZ-403 no clipped whist columns/numbers visual contract
evidence_matrix.scenario: Score/result columns and whist numbers remain visible after the MZ-403 compact score visual fix.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Side score cards pass, but the central settlement score-change table is clipped at laptop width.

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Active fixture uses deterministic non-zero score data for visual readability.
Desktop is readable, but laptop clips the result table.
No screenshot-diff baseline exists; verification is manual screenshot review plus Playwright metrics.
Covered 1280x800 and 1024x768 only, matching the task's desktop/laptop scope; full mobile is out of scope.
Automated tests/build passed, but the manual visual release gate fails due to clipped laptop settlement content.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: PERSONAL-TASK-1158 scope; local static Vite app visual verification only
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: No auth, backend, network, or sensitive data path is involved in this visual-regression task.

downstream_notes: The capture metrics reported no document-level horizontal overflow at 1024px, so the blocker appears to be internal clipping/insufficient responsive behavior in the settlement result table/panel rather than page-wide overflow.

Existing MZ-403 screenshots remain useful supporting evidence for 1280-width desktop, but MZ-406 fresh artifacts should be used for the current visual verdict because they include laptop coverage and a true active score state.
